title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A student at Sandwich Middle High School allegedly made a specific threat against two classmates, prompting an investigation.
The school safety app GoGuardian flagged threatening language, leading to police and school officials' swift response.
The student involved has been removed from school, and the investigation continues with the Cape & Islands District Attorney's Office.

title: Man convicted after breaking into the wrong Boston apartment and murdering resident
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Victor Arrington, 40, was convicted of first-degree murder for a 2015 Dorchester home invasion, resulting in a life sentence.
Prosecutors used iPhone location data as evidence against Arrington, whose first trial ended in a mistrial in 2024.
Arrington and accomplices mistakenly targeted Richard Long's apartment, killing Long and injuring his fiancée before fleeing.

title: Some 20 New Englanders are on ‘American Ninja Warrior’ this season. Here’s your in-depth preview.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Season 18 of 'American Ninja Warrior' features approximately 18 New England contestants, with 12 from Massachusetts.
The Eastern Regional qualifiers start June 29, with the final scheduled for July 13 on NBC.
Ninjas Addy Herman and Noah Meunier are standout contestants, having previously set performance records.

title: Scotland came to Boston. Did romance follow?
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Scottish soccer fans have been visiting Boston for the World Cup, creating a lively atmosphere in the city.
The Haven, a Scottish restaurant in Boston, was popular among Scottish fans during the World Cup soccer match.
Meredith Goldstein's Love Letters explores potential World Cup romances between locals and visiting Scottish tourists.

title: 4 of the weirdest, most absurd soccer movies streaming now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Shaolin Soccer' blends soccer with Shaolin kung fu, offering a comedic take on the sport. Available on Paramount+, Tubi, and more.
'Diamantino' is a political satire featuring a Portuguese soccer star, available on Kanopy and Hoopla.
'United Passions,' a historically low-grossing film with a $30 million budget, can be streamed on Tubi and The Roku Channel.

title: Is the Trump administration on the verge of further endangering North Atlantic right whales?
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Experts are concerned about potential deregulation that may eliminate maritime slow zones critical for the protection of North Atlantic right whales.
North Atlantic right whale population is approximately 380, with vessel strikes posing a significant threat to their survival.
NOAA is considering replacing static seasonal slow zones with dynamic, technology-driven alternatives to reduce economic burdens.

title: Payton Tolle gets standing ovation after tossing seven scoreless innings vs. Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Payton Tolle pitched seven scoreless innings for the Red Sox against the Yankees, striking out seven and allowing one hit.
Tolle carried a perfect game bid into the sixth inning before losing it, despite battling an illness before the game.
Tolle's performance helped Boston to lead the series 2-0 against New York at Fenway Park, with his ERA now at 2.78.

title: 5 ways Celtics could improve lineup without trading Jaylen Brown
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Celtics consider trading for Trey Murphy by offering Derrick White to improve roster without losing Jaylen Brown.
Boston may seek a center upgrade with options like Mitchell Robinson or Zach Collins via trade or free agency.
Offloading Sam Hauser's $10 million contract could provide flexibility for adding a big-name player to the roster.

title: 5 things to know about new Bruins forward JJ Peterka
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins acquired forward JJ Peterka from Utah Mammoth for two first-round picks, including a conditional 2028 selection.
Peterka, a 24-year-old German winger, averaged 26.6 goals over the past three NHL seasons.
Peterka's background includes being named the Best Forward in the 2023 IIHF World Championship for Germany.

title: NBA Rumor Roundup: The latest reports on the Jaylen Brown trade offers
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics open to trade offers for Jaylen Brown, asking for four first-round picks.
Portland Trail Blazers interested in Brown, with potential trade involving young players and draft picks.
Detroit Pistons considering a sign-and-trade for Brown with Jalen Duren once free agency begins.
